result:
Confirmed: 35 whole functions in `sae.c` + 14 whole functions in `ieee802_11.c` = 49 whole, plus 9 partial functions. Total 58 functions across 8 `.c` files. Now I have everything needed to present the final answer.

## CONFIG_SAE — functions whose compiled behavior depends on it (src/ tree)

**Scope note:** exhaustive `grep -rn CONFIG_SAE src/` found only **32 raw hits** (16 ifdef/endif/else pairs) in src/ — far fewer than the ~1985 total `#ifdef CONFIG_*` blocks project-wide. Beyond in-file `#ifdef`, `wpa_supplicant/Makefile` also gates a whole source file (`ifdef CONFIG_SAE → OBJS += ../src/common/sae.o`), which the ccodegraph skill's blind-spot note flags as a second, file-level gating mechanism — included below. Two struct-only `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` blocks (`hostapd.h:268-273`, `sta_info.h:162-164`) contain no functions and are excluded.

### "whole" — entire function only exists under CONFIG_SAE (49 functions)

**`src/common/sae.c` (35/35 functions — whole file is Makefile-gated, no internal `#ifdef` needed):**
sae_set_group, sae_clear_temp_data, sae_clear_data, buf_shift_right, sae_get_rand, sae_get_rand_and_mask, sae_pwd_seed_key, get_rand_1_to_p_1, is_quadratic_residue_blind, sae_test_pwd_seed_ecc, sae_test_pwd_seed_ffc, get_random_qr_qnr, sae_derive_pwe_ecc, sae_derive_pwe_ffc, sae_derive_commit_element_ecc, sae_derive_commit_element_ffc, sae_derive_commit, sae_prepare_commit, sae_derive_k_ecc, sae_derive_k_ffc, sae_derive_keys, sae_process_commit, sae_write_commit, sae_group_allowed, sae_parse_commit_token, sae_parse_commit_scalar, sae_parse_commit_element_ecc, sae_parse_commit_element_ffc, sae_parse_commit_element, sae_parse_commit, sae_cn_confirm, sae_cn_confirm_ecc, sae_cn_confirm_ffc, sae_write_confirm, sae_check_confirm.

**`src/ap/ieee802_11.c` (14 functions, all inside one `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` block, lines 318–876):**
auth_build_sae_commit, auth_build_sae_confirm, auth_sae_send_commit, auth_sae_send_confirm, use_sae_anti_clogging, check_sae_token, auth_build_token_req, sae_check_big_sync, auth_sae_retransmit_timer, sae_clear_retransmit_timer, sae_set_retransmit_timer, sae_sm_step, handle_auth_sae, auth_sae_init_committed.

  - Special nuance on `sae_clear_retransmit_timer`: `src/ap/ieee802_11.h:97-105` declares it with `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` → real prototype (matching the definition above), `#else` → a `static inline` no-op stub body defined right in the header. So the *name* always resolves, but its real logic is 100% CONFIG_SAE-gated — still counted as "whole".

### "partial" — function always compiled, but part of its body is conditional (9 functions)

| Function | File | ifdef line span | What's gated |
|---|---|---|---|
| `handle_auth` | src/ap/ieee802_11.c | 954–957 | one `||` clause admitting `WLAN_AUTH_SAE` into the accepted-auth-alg check |
| `handle_auth` | src/ap/ieee802_11.c | 1180–1200 | `case WLAN_AUTH_SAE:` switch arm calling `handle_auth_sae()` |
| `check_assoc_ies` | src/ap/ieee802_11.c | 1538–1560 | SAE PMKSA-caching / AKM validation checks |
| `ap_free_sta` | src/ap/sta_info.c | 313–316 | freeing `sta->sae` (`sae_clear_data` + `os_free`) |
| `hostapd_ctrl_iface_sta_mib` | src/ap/ctrl_iface_ap.c | 148–155 | emitting `sae_group=` in STA MIB ctrl-iface output |
| `wpa_write_rsn_ie` | src/ap/wpa_auth_ie.c | 191–202 | adding `RSN_AUTH_KEY_MGMT_SAE` / `FT_SAE` AKM suites to the RSNE |
| `wpa_validate_wpa_ie` | src/ap/wpa_auth_ie.c | 524–529 **and** 606–611 | selecting/accepting the SAE and FT-SAE AKM selectors during RSNE validation (two separate blocks in the same function) |
| `hostapd_wpa_auth_get_psk` | src/ap/wpa_auth_glue.c | 231–237 | short-circuit returning `sta->sae->pmk` for SAE-authenticated stations |
| `rsn_key_mgmt_to_bitfield` | src/common/wpa_common.c | 485–490 | mapping `RSN_AUTH_KEY_MGMT_SAE`/`FT_SAE` selectors to `WPA_KEY_MGMT_*` bits |
| `wpa_gen_wpa_ie_rsn` | src/rsn_supp/wpa_ie.c | 173–178 | emitting the SAE / FT-SAE AKM selector when generating an RSNE |

(`handle_auth` and `wpa_validate_wpa_ie` each have two separate ifdef blocks but count once as a function.)

### Total: **58 functions** depend on `CONFIG_SAE` — 49 whole, 9 partial, across 6 `.c` files (`sae.c`, `ieee802_11.c`, `sta_info.c`, `ctrl_iface_ap.c`, `wpa_auth_ie.c`, `wpa_auth_glue.c`, `wpa_common.c`, `wpa_ie.c` — 8 files total).

Checked-but-ruled-out: `wpa_key_mgmt_sae()` (src/common/defs.h) and `wpa_auth_uses_sae()`/`wpa_auth_uses_ft_sae()` (src/ap/wpa_auth.c) are called from several of the partial blocks above but are themselves unconditional — no `CONFIG_SAE` text appears in their files.
